text: Day 1 rule: if a required subsystem is fundamentally unwinnable on the floor, scrap the unit.
details: Programming: day 1 commands are straightforward. If the output can be repaired, patch it and keep the unit moving. If the command path is dead on arrival, scrap it.
Gears: a cracked input drive or cracked output drive is a hard fail. That drivetrain cannot be saved on the floor.
Wires: day 1 only uses plain power lines. If the first live lead is physically missing, the machine is disqualified and must be scrapped.
Circuit Board: day 1 boards can use exact grouped totals and simple = links. If a charge cell is boxed into dead space or the board cannot satisfy the required links, scrap it.

text: Day 2 rule: scrap any unit that breaks compliance, even if the rest of the machine still runs.
details: Programming: outputs may drift or come back protocol-wrong. Invalid, corrupted, or noncompliant returns are scrap signals.
Gears: rust starts appearing and rejection-marked parts can enter the train. If the bad part is unavoidable, scrap the machine.
Wires: green and orange filters recolor the route after that tile. If a required module is forced red or receives the wrong power class, scrap it.
Circuit Board: day 2 adds < regions on top of the earlier checks. Broken glyph markers and isolated charge cells are both compliance failures.

text: Day 3 rule: hazardous instability overrides everything else. If it sparks, crackles, or contaminates the system, scrap it.
details: Programming: the green debugger bug with red sparks is hazardous contamination, not a normal mismatch. Scrap that unit immediately.
Gears: deadlock clamps create real dead space and red spark instability marks unsafe drivetrain discharge. Any live spark state is scrap.
Wires: day 3 expands to multiple sources. Neutral, green, and orange are valid feeds. Red crackling outputs are never safe and must be scrapped.
Circuit Board: day 3 mixes exact groups, < regions, = links, and != links. Earlier scrap states still count, and any corrupted red domino means automatic scrap.

text: QUEST: The shady umbrella wants you to help start a rebellion against the machine line.
details: Unlocked through a private comms proposition from the Rebellious Umbrella.
This is a story lead, not a payroll directive. Keep an ear on the comms channel.
If the line starts talking about rebellion again, check the chat feed before filing your ruling.
